comment.body:
bcs you bought the asset for an ask price, but can sell it for the bid that is lower.
bcs you must pay transaction fees for your broker, per transaction!
aaand... and...
if you have any real win (not just on paper, but above the bid/ask spread and fees) you must pay taxes after your gain.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
bcs lets say, your (or your followed furu's) directional accuracy rate is 60%
and lets say, the quant algos directional accuracy rate is only about 45%

the algo will make like 100 trades in a week (i said a laughable low number now)
it will not be 45 win and 55 loss, but it will converge... maybe like 30 win and 70 loss, or 60 win and only 40 loss during these 100 trades, but the key is, the more trades it takes, the more the win rate converges to 45%
and thats what its sizing algo was programmed to manage (logoptimalization)

if it has a winning streak producing 60 wins vs 40 losses over 100 trades, I may stop the algo and take the profit...
or if it has more red trades, I let it run and i know that it will produce a positive average return the more trades it makes

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
now, and there is you with your way higher dir. accuracy rate of 60%
you make 20 trades a month, or 10 trades in a week...
you lose 8 times, and win 2 times.
next week, similar thing happens.
lets say you did 60 trades a month, 3 trades per day.

and you have 40 losses and 20 winning. 
or 30-30,
or 20-40 whatever...
lets say, your sizing is uneducated, and you sized every trades the same.

you will be negative at the end of the month.
but the key is: you didn't even take 100 trades... you are driven by emotions, you cannot make quick and purely logical decisions like an algo does by computing velocity, mean reversion probabilities etc. so quickly, adjusting stop-losses and position sizings etc.
you are maybe just in a losing streak with oversized positions, your edge never started to work
